qa_pairs.question: What are the available GPU instance sizes when using Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology on Nibi?
qa_pairs.answer: When using MIG technology, Nibi offers three GPU instance sizes: H100-1g.10gb, H100-2g.20gb, and H100-3g.40gb.

qa_pairs.question: What are the Slurm options for requesting full H100-80gb GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one H100-80gb GPU, use `--gpus=h100:1` or `--gpus=h100_80gb:1`. For multiple H100-80gb GPUs per node, use `--gpus-per-node=h100:2`, `--gpus-per-node=h100:3`, or `--gpus-per-node=h100:4`. For multiple full H100 GPUs spread anywhere, use `--gpus=h100:n` (replacing 'n' with the desired number of GPUs).

qa_pairs.question: What is the computing power and memory of an H100-1g.10gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: An H100-1g.10gb GPU instance offers 1/8th of the computing power with 10GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What is the computing power and memory of an H100-2g.20gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: An H100-2g.20gb GPU instance offers 2/8th of the computing power with 20GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What is the computing power and memory of an H100-3g.40gb GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: An H100-3g.40gb GPU instance offers 3/8th of the computing power with 40GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request a single H100-1g.10gb GPU instance for a compute job?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one H100-1g.10gb GPU instance, use the Slurm option `--gpus=h100_1g.10gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request a single H100-2g.20gb GPU instance for a compute job?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one H100-2g.20gb GPU instance, use the Slurm option `--gpus=h100_2g.20gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request a single H100-3g.40gb GPU instance for a compute job?
qa_pairs.answer: To request one H100-3g.40gb GPU instance, use the Slurm option `--gpus=h100_3g.40gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: Do the nodes on Nibi have Internet access?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, all nodes on Nibi have Internet access.

qa_pairs.question: Is special firewall permission or proxying required for Internet access on Nibi?
qa_pairs.answer: No, special firewall permission or proxying is not necessary for Internet access on Nibi.

qa_pairs.question: Are user directories created by default on `/project`?
qa_pairs.answer: No, user directories are no longer created by default on `/project`.

qa_pairs.question: How can users create directories in their group's `/project` space?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can create their own directories in their group's `/project` using the `mkdir` command.

qa_pairs.question: What is the benefit of allowing groups to organize their own `/project` space?
qa_pairs.answer: This allows groups to decide how their `/project` is organized for sharing data among group members.

qa_pairs.question: What is the soft quota for users on `/scratch`?
qa_pairs.answer: Each user has a 1 TB soft quota on `/scratch`.

qa_pairs.question: How long can a user exceed the soft quota on `/scratch`?
qa_pairs.answer: The soft quota on `/scratch` can be exceeded for up to 60 days.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after the 60-day grace period if the `/scratch` soft quota is still exceeded?
qa_pairs.answer: After the 60-day grace period expires, no additional files may be written to `/scratch`.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user write files to `/scratch` again after exceeding the soft limit and the grace period?
qa_pairs.answer: Files can be written again once the user has removed or deleted enough files to bring their total scratch usage under 1 TB.

qa_pairs.question: What is Open OnDemand (OOD) and how does it help access the Nibi cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Open OnDemand (OOD) is a web-based platform that simplifies cluster access by providing a web interface to the login nodes and a remote desktop environment, allowing users to access Nibi through a web browser.

qa_pairs.question: What is the web interface for Nibi's Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: The web interface for Nibi's Open OnDemand is https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: What options are available after logging into the Nibi Open OnDemand interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Upon logging into the Nibi Open OnDemand interface, users will see options to open a Bash shell terminal or launch a remote desktop session.

qa_pairs.question: Can JupyterLab be run interactively via Nibi Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, JupyterLab can be run interactively via the Nibi Open OnDemand portal.
